title: AgentEvaluationInput
description: Standard input schema for the prompt refinement and evaluation tool. Captures everything needed to evaluate an agent's system prompt and guide its refinement.

agent.description: The agent whose system prompt will be refined.

description.description: Brief summary of the agent's purpose.
examples: Explains code in plain English for beginners

system_prompt.description: The current system prompt to be evaluated and refined.

model.description: Model identifier the agent runs on.

model_parameters.description: Model-level configuration that may influence prompt design.

tools.description: Tools/functions the agent can call. The system prompt often references these, so the refinement tool needs to know about them.

name.description: Tool name as the model sees it.

parameters.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters.

output_format.description: The format the agent is expected to produce.

output_schema.description: When output_format is 'json', the JSON Schema the agent's output should conform to. For example, the resume info collector outputs a specific JSON structure.

test_cases.description: Scenarios used to evaluate prompt quality. Without these, refinement is blind — they define what 'better' means.

description.description: What this test case is checking.

input.description: The user message sent to the agent.

expected_output.description: A reference 'ideal' output for exact or similarity comparison.

expected_behavior.description: A natural-language description of what the agent should do. Use this when an exact expected_output isn't practical.
examples: Should identify the off-by-one error in the loop
Should return valid JSON with all required fields populated

context.description: Additional context for this test case, such as conversation history or outputs from upstream agents in a pipeline.

evaluation_criteria.description: What the refinement tool should optimise toward.

constraints.description: Hard rules the refined prompt must still satisfy.

priority_description.description: Free-text explanation of what matters most and any trade-offs.
examples: Accuracy is more important than brevity. The agent should never guess — if unsure, it should say so.
